meta.title: Morphology control and low-temperature magnetotransport in chiral 2D perovskite R-(MBA)2PbI4
meta.notes: Extraction transcribes checkable quantitative claims; figure-read values (XRD intensity, Hall slope, VRH R^2 fits) noted with reduced confidence.

claims.text: Hall measurements under dark conditions identify p-type conduction with a Hall mobility of ~0.2 cm2 V-1 s-1.

location.quote: unambiguously identifying p-type conduction with a Hall mobility of ∼ 0.2cm2V

claims.text: Carrier density of ~3×10^14 cm-2.

location.quote: a carrier density of ∼ 3×1014 cm−2

claims.text: At room temperature the measured Hall coefficient corresponds to a mobility µH = 0.207 cm2/V·s and a sheet carrier density p = 3.29×10^14 cm-2.

location.quote:
the measured Hall coefficient corresponds to a mobility µH = 0.207 cm2/V·s and a sheet
carrier density p = 3.29 × 1014 cm−2

claims.text: R4000rpm/R1500rpm ≈ 850 at 1.5 V, much larger than the ~threefold increase expected from thickness alone.

location.quote:
the
much larger observed increase (R4000rpm/R1500rpm ≈ 850 at 1.5V)

claims.scope: 1.5 V, comparing 4000 rpm and 1500 rpm films

claims.text: Thickness alone would predict only an approximately threefold increase in resistance between the 1500 rpm and 4000 rpm films.

location.quote:
thickness alone would
predict only an approximately threefold increase in resistance between the 1500 rpm and
4000 rpm films if the resistivity were unchanged

claims.text: 1500 rpm film had average thickness ~300 nm and the lowest measured resistance (~10 MΩ).

location.quote: the resulting film had an average thickness of ∼ 300 nm (Figure 1a) and exhibited large,

claims.text: Films thicknesses: 2000 rpm ~225 nm, 3000 rpm ~165 nm, 4000 rpm ~100 nm.

location.quote:
produced thinner films (∼225 nm) with slightly
smaller grains and reduced domain connectivity

claims.text: Two Arrhenius regimes with activation energies of 0.11 eV for T > 140 K and 0.015 eV for T < 70 K.

location.quote: at high temperatures (T > 140 K), the slope yields Ea ≈ 0.11 eV, whereas at low temperatures (T < 70 K), Ea ≈ 0.015 eV is extracted

claims.predicate: E_a == slope * k_B from R(T)=R0 exp(Ea/(kB T)) fit
claims.scope: T>140 K and T<70 K regimes

claims.text: In-plane resistance measurements were performed down to 38 K, the lowest temperature reported for in-plane transport in this material; prior chiral-HOIP FET studies reported down to 77-78 K.

location.quote: in prior temperature-dependent chiralHOIP FET studies measurements were reported down to 77–78 K

claims.text: FET MR: large positive MR of 10% at 3T at room temperature, and ~-9% at 3T at 78.2 K.

location.quote:
Large positive MR of 10% at 3T is observed at room-temperature, whereas large negative
MR of approximately -9% at 3T is observed at 78.2 K

claims.text: Hall-bar paths crossing the grain boundary exhibit MR of 6-8%, while paths avoiding it exhibit MR of 0.05-0.2%.

location.quote:
measurement paths that traversed
across the observed grain boundary exhibit a much larger MR of 6-8%, comparable with
that observed in the FET sample. However, the low resistance current paths that avoided
this boundary exhibited a much smaller MR in the range of 0.05-0.2%

claims.text: At 1V, paths avoiding the grain boundary showed resistances ~10-50 MΩ, paths crossing showed ~1.5 GΩ, an increase of roughly two orders of magnitude.

location.quote: At 1V, paths avoiding the grain boundary showed resistances of approximately 10–50 MΩ, whereas paths crossing the boundary showed resistances

claims.text: Hall-bar geometry is expected to be approximately 500 times more resistive than the three-terminal channel for the same film thickness and resistivity.

location.quote:
the Hall-bar geometry is expected to be
approximately 500 times more resistive than the three-terminal channel for the same film
thickness and resistivity

claims.text: Through tuning of spin-coating speed, the in-plane resistivity was reduced by more than three orders of magnitude.

location.quote:
the in-plane resistivity was reduced by more than three
orders of magnitude, enabling reliable lateral device operation

claims.text: The Hall voltage VH(B) varies linearly with B over the full range and its positive slope confirms p-type transport.

location.quote:
VH(B) varies linearly
with B over the full range, and the positive
slope of VH confirms p-type transport in the
film

claims.predicate: V_H(B) linear in B with positive slope -> p-type

claims.text: Comparable Hall mobility values reported in related 2D perovskites; undoped 2D lead-iodide perovskites such as (PEA)2PbI4 typically exhibit mobilities ~0.1-1 cm2/V·s.

location.quote: undoped 2D lead-iodide perovskites such as (PEA)2PbI4 typically exhibit mobilities on the order of ∼0.1–1 cm2/V·s

claims.predicate: measured_mu (0.207) within reported range 0.1-1 cm2/V·s for (PEA)2PbI4

claims.text: Contact resistance extracted from multi-terminal structures averaged ~50 kΩ and was negligible compared to the film resistance.

location.quote:
The contact resistance extracted from multi-terminal structures averaged ∼ 50 kΩ and was
negligible compared to the film resistance

claims.text: Three and four-wire measurements showed contact resistance never exceeded 5% for any current path.

location.quote:
Three and four-wire measurements showed that contact resistance never exceeded 5% for
any current path

claims.text: These measurements represent the first Hall-effect characterization of R-(MBA)2PbI4 thin films under dark conditions.

location.quote:
these measurements represent
the first Hall-effect characterization of R-(MBA)2PbI4 thin films under dark conditions

claims.predicate: novelty claim: first dark Hall-effect characterization of R-(MBA)2PbI4

claims.text: VRH fits did not conclusively distinguish a VRH model from Arrhenius-type transport; linear fit R^2 values: T^-1 0.9866, T^-1/2 0.9876, T^-1/3 0.9887, T^-1/4 0.9893.

location.quote:
the present data do not show an obvious improvement in linearization in this regime compared with the Arrhenius plot, and thus do not
conclusively distinguish a VRH mechanism from Arrhenius-type transport

claims.predicate: R^2 comparison across T^-1, T^-1/2, T^-1/3, T^-1/4 fits does not favor VRH

claims.text: Films prepared at spin speeds above 1000 rpm show a strong (002) diffraction peak; the 1500 rpm film gives the highest intensity.

location.quote:
with films spun above
1000 rpm showing clear (002) reflections, with the 1500 rpm film giving the highest intensity

claims.predicate: (002) peak intensity maximal for 1500 rpm in XRD

claims.text: Lead (II) iodide 413 mg, 0.895 mmol; (R)-(-)-α-methylbenzylamine 228 µL, 1.80 mmol used in synthesis.

location.quote: lead (II) iodide (413 mg, 0.895 mmol; Sigma Aldrich)

claims.predicate: mmol == mass/molar_mass; PbI2: 413mg/461.01 ≈ 0.895 mmol; MBA molar ratio

claims.text: Magnitude of low-field MR in devices (|ΔR|/R ~ 10% at |B| ~ 1 T) is comparable to that reported for other hybrid lead-halide perovskites.

location.quote:
the magnitude of the low-field MR in our devices (|∆R|/R ∼ 10% at
|B|∼ 1 T) is comparable to that reported for other hybrid lead–halide perovskites

claims.predicate: measured_MR (~10% at 1T) comparable to literature values for hybrid lead-halide perovskites
